title: 10 best herbal creams for athlete's foot

remedy_properties: Tea Tree Oil (Melaleuca Alternifolia): Has antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which help to combat fungal infections like athlete's foot by inhibiting the growth of pathogens.

Aloe Vera: Possesses anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help to reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with athlete's foot, while also promoting wound healing and skin hydration.

Calendula (Marigold): Has antimicrobial and antiseptic properties that aid in the treatment of fungal infections like athlete's foot by inhibiting the growth of fungi and promoting tissue regeneration.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree, creams helps with athlete's foot because of its antimicrobial properties, which effectively combat fungal infections. The active compound, terpinen-4-ol, has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, including those that cause athlete's foot. Additionally, tea tree oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness, itching, and discomfort associated with the condition. By applying tea tree oil cream to the affected area, individuals can promote faster healing and reduce the risk of future outbreaks.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): Acts as an antifungal and antibacterial agent, helping to eliminate fungal and bacterial infections that cause athlete's foot.
Methyl Eugenol: Possesses antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the affected skin, reducing redness and itching associated with athlete's foot.
Terpinen-4-ol: Exhibits potent antifungal activity, inhibiting the growth of fungi that cause athlete's foot, while also promoting the healing of skin lesions and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high oil content, particularly terpinen-4-ol, which has antifungal properties.
Barks: The barks are used for their antimicrobial properties, which help to prevent the growth of fungi that cause athlete's foot.
Stems: The stems are also used for their oil content, including terpinen-4-ol, which contributes to the antimicrobial and antifungal properties of the creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30g of dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves from a trusted supplier or grow them yourself in a controlled environment.
Combine the dried leaves with 100ml of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 hours or overnight to allow the herbal properties to infuse into the oil.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container to remove the solids.
Mix the infused oil with 20-30% beeswax and 10-20% emulsifier to create a smooth and stable cream.

remedy_properties: Soothing and anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's gel contains compounds like aloin, aloe-emodin, and saponins, which help to reduce inflammation, redness, and swelling associated with athlete's foot.

Antifungal and antibacterial properties: The aloe vera plant contains compounds like salicylic acid, urea, and other phenolic acids that have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi and bacteria that cause athlete's foot.
Moisturizing and emollient properties: Aloe vera gel helps to lock in moisture and soothe dry, itchy skin, reducing the discomfort and irritation associated with athlete's foot.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with athlete's foot because of its antifungal and antibacterial properties. The cream's soothing and moisturizing effects help to calm and soften the skin, reducing irritation and inflammation caused by fungal infections. Additionally, aloe vera's natural compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal cells, thereby helping to combat the symptoms of athlete's foot and promote healing. This makes aloe vera a popular and effective remedy for this common condition.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: Aloe-emodin, a lignan, has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce fungal growth and alleviate symptoms associated with athlete's foot.
Cinnamaldehyde: Cinnamaldehyde, a phenolic compound, possesses antifungal properties that can inhibit the growth of fungi responsible for athlete's foot, such as Trichophyton and Epidermophyton.

Aloe-β-sitosterol: Aloe-β-sitosterol, a phytosterol, has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe and protect the skin from further irritation caused by athlete's foot.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera gel, extracted from the leaves, is used in creams for athlete's foot due to its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties.
Gel (from Leaves): The gel is used in creams for athlete's foot as it has antifungal properties that help combat fungal infections.
Juice (from Leaves): The juice is used in creams for athlete's foot due to its antiseptic and antibacterial properties that aid in wound healing and infection prevention.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 ounces of fresh aloe vera gel and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a mixing bowl.
Blend 2 ounces of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in a separate saucepan over low heat.
Combine the blended mixture with the aloe vera gel and shea butter in the mixing bowl.
Add 2 teaspoons of vitamin e oil and 10 drops of lavender essential oil to the mixture.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and refrigerate for 30 minutes before applying.

remedy_properties: Antibacterial and antifungal activity: Pot marigold contains compounds like flavonoids and terpenes that exhibit antimicrobial properties, helping to combat fungal infections like athlete's foot.
Anti-inflammatory effects: The cream's active ingredients have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with athlete's foot.
Antioxidant activity: Pot marigold contains antioxidants that help protect the skin from further damage caused by free radicals, promoting healing and reducing the risk of infection.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with athlete's foot because of its antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in Calendula officinalis, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, work together to reduce fungal growth and soothe irritated skin. Calendula cream creates a barrier on the skin's surface, preventing the fungus from entering and causing further infection. This natural remedy also helps to reduce inflammation, itchiness, and discomfort associated with athlete's foot, making it a popular alternative treatment for this common condition.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce inflammation, prevent the growth of fungi, and promote healing of the affected skin.

Phenolic acids (especially caffeic acid and ferulic acid): These acids exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, which inhibit the growth of fungi and bacteria, reduce oxidative stress, and promote tissue repair.

Flavonoids (especially quercetin and kaempferol): These compounds possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce inflammation, prevent the growth of fungi, and promote wound healing and tissue repair.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are primarily used due to their high content of triterpenoid saponins and flavonoids, which have anti-fungal and anti-inflammatory properties beneficial for treating athlete's foot.
Leaves: Leaves are used for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help in soothing and treating fungal infections such as athlete's foot.
Seeds : Seeds are not used frequently for athlete's foot treatment, however, seeds of Calendula officinalis are used in some herbal remedies and creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 calendula flowers at peak bloom for optimal potency and effectiveness in your herbal cream.
Infuse 1 cup of dried calendula flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain a clear oil extract.
Mix the calendula oil extract with beeswax and shea butter in a ratio of 1 part calendula to 2 parts wax and butter.
Pour the mixture into a double boiler and heat until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture is smooth and creamy.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Kansas Coneflower contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as kaempferol and quercetin, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with Athlete's Foot.

Antifungal agents: The cream may contain antifungal compounds like triterpenoids, which can inhibit the growth of fungi that cause Athlete's Foot, reducing symptoms such as itching, burning, and cracking.
Antioxidants: Kansas Coneflower's antioxidant properties can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healing and reducing the appearance of inflammation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia, also known as Kansas coneflower, creams helps with athlete's foot because of its antifungal properties, which combat the fungal infections that cause the condition. The cream's antimicrobial properties also help to reduce inflammation and promote a healthy environment for the skin to recover. By applying the cream directly to the affected area, individuals can experience relief from the itching, burning, and discomfort associated with athlete's foot. Phenolic Acids: Phenolic acids, particularly caffeic acid and ferulic acid, have been shown to possess antifungal and antibacterial activities, which can aid in controlling the spread of athlete's foot.

Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids in Echinacea angustifolia may have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms such as redness, itching, and irritation associated with athlete's foot.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Echinacea angustifolia are used to make creams for athlete's foot due to their antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Echinacea angustifolia are used to make creams for athlete's foot due to their antiseptic and antimicrobial properties that help combat fungal infections.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Echinacea angustifolia are used to make creams for athlete's foot due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest a sufficient quantity of echinacea angustifolia roots with clean gardening tools in late summer or early fall.
Dry the roots in a warm dry place with good air circulation for 7 to 10 days.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the powder with a carrier oil such as coconut oil in a 1:4 ratio by weight and heat the mixture to 150 degrees fahrenheit for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve and transfer the resulting cream to a glass jar for storage.

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, which helps to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with athlete's foot.

Isoliquiritigenin: This flavonoid has been shown to exhibit antifungal activity against a range of fungal species, including those that cause athlete's foot, helping to combat infection and promote healing.
Licoricidin: This compound has antibacterial properties, which can help to prevent bacterial infections from occurring in the affected area, further reducing inflammation and discomfort.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a reputable source.
Infuse 20 grams of the dried roots in 500 milliliters of boiling water for 15 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl and discard the solids.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat.
Combine the cooled infusion with the melted wax and oil mixture and whip until smooth.

remedy_properties: Antifungal activity: Dandelion extracts have been shown to inhibit the growth of various fungal species, including those that cause athlete's foot, reducing symptoms such as itching and cracking.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Dandelion contains compounds like taraxasterol, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with athlete's foot.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers from a pesticide-free area for 30 minutes.
Combine 1/2 cup of the harvested taraxacum officinale with 1/2 cup of distilled water in a saucepan.
Simmer the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes while reducing to 1/4 cup for 20 minutes.
Strain the liquid and discard the solids then add 1/4 cup of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil.
Stir the mixture until the beeswax is fully melted and the cream has cooled to room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Chamomile creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like apigenin and luteolin, which help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with athlete's foot.

Antifungal properties: Chamomile has been shown to exhibit antifungal activity against certain fungi that cause fungal infections, including those that can lead to athlete's foot, such as Trichophyton and Epidermophyton species.

remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, which help soothe and treat athlete's foot.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated completely.
Steep 100g of the dried flowers in 1l of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to make a strong infusion.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain a clear liquid.
Combine 500ml of the clear liquid with 50g of beeswax and 20g of emulsifying wax in a heat-proof bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes to achieve a smooth and consistent cream texture.

remedy_properties: Antifungal Properties: Asiatic Pennywort has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal organisms, including those that cause Athlete's Foot (tinea pedis), by disrupting their cell membranes and preventing them from producing toxins.
Anti-inflammatory Properties: The creams' anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with Athlete's Foot, providing relief and comfort to affected areas.

Wound-Healing Properties: Asiatic Pennywort has been found to enhance wound healing by promoting collagen synthesis, improving tissue oxygenation, and reducing bacterial colonization, which helps to accelerate the recovery process from Athlete's Foot.

The active compounds in Centella asiatica, such as asiatic acid and madecassic acid, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi and bacteria that cause athlete's foot. These compounds also possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing discomfort and promoting healthy skin regeneration. remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids, particularly asiatic acid and madecassic acid, have antifungal properties that help combat fungal infections that cause athlete's foot.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh centella asiatica leaves and flowers at dawn or late afternoon to ensure highest potency.
Dry the harvested centella asiatica in a cool dark place for 3-5 days to remove excess moisture.
Combine 50g of dried centella asiatica with 100g of coconut oil and 20g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30-40 minutes or until it reaches 160-180°f to melt the beeswax.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and fill into a clean container for storage.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Stinging nettle cream has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi and bacteria, including those that cause athlete's foot, due to its ability to disrupt cell membranes and prevent microbial multiplication.

Polyphenols: Polyphenols, a type of phenolic compound found in Urtica dioica, exhibit anti-inflammatory and antifungal activities that aid in soothing and healing athlete's foot symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 fresh urtica dioica leaves and stems for their medicinal properties in late spring or early summer.
Clean the harvested urtica dioica thoroughly with distilled water and filter out any debris or dirt.
Steam distill 100g of the cleaned urtica dioica leaves and stems for 30 minutes to release their essential oils.
Mix 20g of the obtained urtica dioica essential oil with 80g of a carrier oil such as coconut oil or olive oil.
Combine the urtica dioica oil mixture with 100g of a moisturizing base such as shea butter or beeswax and 20g of a preservative.

Barks: They are used in creams for athlete's foot due to their antifungal properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of vaccinium macrocarpon berries when they are ripe and in season.
Dry the berries in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 6 hours to preserve their properties.
Steep 1 tablespoon of dried vaccinium macrocarpon in 1 cup of hot water for 5 minutes.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the vaccinium macrocarpon infusion with 2 ounces of beeswax and 2 ounces of coconut oil.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring constantly, until the beeswax is fully incorporated and melted.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder creams helps with athlete's foot because they contain antifungal properties that effectively combat fungal infections that cause this common condition. remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried sambucus nigra leaves and crush them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the crushed sambucus nigra powder with 50 milliliters of cold-pressed coconut oil in a double boiler at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Add 20 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of vitamin e oil to the mixture and stir until fully incorporated over a period of 5 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to around 60 degrees celsius before whipping with an electric mixer for 2 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and store them in the refrigerator at 3-4 degrees celsius for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with athlete's foot because of its natural antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's soothing effects calm itching and redness, while its antifungal ingredients work to combat the underlying fungal infection. Additionally, lavender's antimicrobial compounds help to reduce the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that can exacerbate athlete's foot. remedy_recipe: Infuse dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil for 2 weeks to extract essential compounds.
Strain the infused oil mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids after 24 hours of settling.
Combine the infused oil with beeswax and shea butter in a double boiler, heating until the mixture reaches 160°f to 180°f for 30 minutes.
Add vitamin e oil and tea tree essential oil in a ratio of 5:1 to the melted mixture, stirring well to combine and cooling to around 120°f before pouring into jars.
Allow the cream to cool and set at room temperature for at least 2 hours before use, resulting in a soothing topical treatment for athlete's foot relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with athlete's foot because of their unique combination of antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi that cause athlete's foot, while also reducing inflammation and itching associated with the condition. Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, including gingerols and paradols, help to reduce inflammation and alleviate redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with athlete's foot infections.

Wound healing properties: Ginger's antioxidant and antibacterial compounds promote wound healing by enhancing collagen synthesis, improving tissue regeneration, and reducing the risk of secondary infections.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and reducing inflammation associated with athlete's foot.
Roots: Containing natural antifungal and antibacterial properties that help combat fungal infections causing athlete's foot, while also providing relief from itching and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather 500 grams of dried ginger root and 250 milliliters of coconut oil with anti-inflammatory properties.
Grind the dried ginger root into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 30 seconds.
Combine the ground ginger powder with 100 milliliters of beeswax and 150 milliliters of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over medium heat for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture reaches a temperature of 120 degrees fahrenheit.
Remove the mixture from heat, let it cool to room temperature, then whip it with a spatula or electric mixer for 2 minutes to create a smooth and creamy consistency.

Soothing and moisturizing properties: Field horsetail's natural silica content helps to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing dryness and flakiness while also providing long-lasting moisture and hydration, which can help prevent further irritation.

Barks: Rich in silica and other minerals, which help to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh equisetum arvense plant material in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the harvested plant material using air drying or shade drying for at least two weeks.
Blend one part dried equisetum arvense with three parts coconut oil and apply heat for 30 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and strain it through a cheesecloth into a clean container for storage.
Whip the cooled mixture with an electric mixer until smooth and creamy for application.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Japanese honeysuckle creams helps with athlete's foot because of its remarkable antifungal and antibacterial properties. The herb's active compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, effectively combat fungal infections like athlete's foot by inhibiting the growth of Candida and other microorganisms that cause the condition. Additionally, the cream's soothing and moisturizing effects help to reduce inflammation, itching, and scaling associated with athlete's foot, promoting healthy skin and a rapid recovery.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Japanese honeysuckle cream contains compounds like beta-amyrin and oleanolic acid, which have been shown to exhibit antibacterial and antifungal activity against various microorganisms that cause athlete's foot, helping to combat the infection.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and saponins, help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with athlete's foot, providing relief and promoting healing.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh lonicera japonica flowers and dry them in the shade for 2 days.
Grind 20g of dried flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 10g of the powdered lonicera japonica with 50g of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat for 1 hour.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it cool to room temperature before mixing in 30g of beeswax pellets and stirring until melted and combined.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars, seal tightly, and store in the refrigerator at a consistent temperature below 40°f for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with athlete's foot because of its natural antifungal and antibacterial properties. The active ingredients in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, work to combat the fungal infection that causes athlete's foot, reducing itching, burning, and cracking on the skin. remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Thyme contains compounds like thymol and carvacrol, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens, such as Candida and Trichophyton, that cause athlete's foot.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and stems, rinsing them gently with cool water.
Combine 100g of dried thymus vulgaris flowers with 400ml of carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 4 hours under the shade, shaking the jar occasionally to enhance extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil.
Mix the infused oil with 100g of beeswax and 50ml of distilled water in a double boiler, heating until the mixture reaches 70°c to create a smooth cream consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with athlete's foot because they contain antifungal properties that target the root cause of the infection. The salvestrols present in sage have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, such as Trichophyton and Microsporum, which are common causes of athlete's foot. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves and dry them thoroughly in shade for 7-10 days.
Grind dried salvia officinalis leaves into fine powder using mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Combine 20g of ground salvia officinalis with 100ml of cold pressed coconut oil and mix until well combined.
Heat the mixture at low temperature for 30 minutes, then strain through cheesecloth to remove solids.
Transfer the filtered cream to sterile jars and store in refrigerator at 4°c for up to 6 months.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of dried goldenseal root hydrastis canadensis powder and sift it through a fine-mesh sieve.
Combine the sifted goldenseal root powder with 100 milliliters of distilled water in a heat-resistant glass bowl.
Soak the mixture in a warm water bath for 30 minutes to allow for optimal extraction of herbal properties.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.
Mix the resulting liquid with 20 grams of petroleum jelly and store it in an airtight container at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fenugreek creams helps with athlete's foot because of its potent antifungal properties, which effectively combat the underlying fungal infection that causes this common condition. remedy_recipe: Harvest trigonella foenum graecum seeds in bulk and dry them properly for later use.
Grind 100 grams of dried trigonella foenum graecum seeds into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered trigonella foenum graecum with 200 grams of coconut oil and 50 grams of beeswax in a heat-resistant bowl.
Heat the mixture slowly over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted.
Pour the cooled cream into sterilized containers and store them in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for athlete's foot are natural remedies that help alleviate the symptoms and treat the fungal infection causing this common foot condition. They work by reducing inflammation, killing fungal spores, and promoting a healthy environment for skin recovery. The best herbal creams for athlete's foot include Melaleuca alternifolia, Eucalyptus globulus, Calendula officinalis, Azadirachta indica, Allium sativum, Aloe barbadensis, Aloe vera, Cymbopogon citratus, Origanum vulgare, and Curcuma longa. If left untreated, athlete's foot can cause significant discomfort, embarrassment, and even lead to more serious infections, impacting daily activities, social life, and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to athlete's foot that are treated with herbal creams are fungal infections such as ringworm, jock itch, and toenail fungus. These conditions are caused by fungi that thrive in warm, moist environments, just like athlete's foot. Herbal creams containing ingredients like tea tree oil, aloe vera, and lavender oil have antifungal properties that help combat these infections, providing relief from itching, redness, and irritation.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with athlete's foot is a blend of Tea Tree Oil, Aloe Vera, and Calendula. Tea Tree Oil has antifungal properties that combat fungal infections, while Aloe Vera soothes and calms irritated skin. Calendula, rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, accelerates the healing process and reduces redness. This trio works together to provide long-lasting relief and promote a healthy, fungus-free environment for the skin to recover. Regular application can lead to significant improvement in athlete's foot symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams helps with athlete's foot because of its potent antimicrobial properties. The active compound, tea tree oil, inhibits the growth of fungi that cause the infection, such as Trichophyton and Epidermophyton. This natural cream soothes and calms the affected skin, reducing inflammation and itching. Regular application can help to clear the infection and prevent further outbreaks, promoting healthy skin and a reduced risk of fungal infections. It's a natural and effective way to treat athlete's foot.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (eucalyptol): Acts as an antimicrobial agent that helps eliminate fungal growth and infection causing athlete's foot.
Terpinen-4-ol: Exhibits antifungal properties that inhibit the growth and reproduction of fungi responsible for athlete's foot, thus providing relief from symptoms.
Linalool: Demonstrates antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which help reduce inflammation and soothe the skin affected by athlete's foot.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high content of tea tree oil, which has antifungal and antibacterial properties to combat fungal infections.
Seeds: Utilized for their oil, which has antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties to reduce redness and itching associated with athlete's foot.
Barks: Employed for their extracts, which have antimicrobial properties to inhibit the growth of fungi and bacteria causing athlete's foot.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 20 grams of dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves and grind them into a fine powder.
Combine 50 grams of beeswax with 20 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler at 60 degrees celsius for 10 minutes.
Add 10 grams of shea butter and 5 grams of vitamin e oil to the beeswax mixture and stir well.
Mix 10 grams of the melaleuca alternifolia powder with 50 grams of distilled water to create a herbal infusion.
Combine the herbal infusion with the beeswax mixture and stir until a smooth and creamy texture is achieved.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams helps with athlete's foot because of its potent antifungal properties, which target and eliminate fungal spores that cause the infection. The menthol and camphor present in the cream also help to reduce inflammation and itching, providing relief to affected skin. Additionally, eucalyptus globulus has natural deodorizing properties that help to eliminate fungal odors and promote a healthy environment, making it an effective treatment for athlete's foot. Leaves (again): The leaves are also used for their astringent and antiseptic properties, which aid in drying out fungal infections and promoting a healthy environment for the skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25g of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves from a reputable supplier to use in your recipe.
Infuse 200ml of carrier oil in a double boiler with 25g of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container to remove the plant material.
Mix 50g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter in a double boiler to melt for 10 minutes.
Combine the infused oil with the melted beeswax and shea butter mixture to create a uniform cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams helps with athlete's foot because of its antifungal and antibacterial properties. The cream, derived from the Calendula plant, contains triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi such as Trichophyton, the primary cause of athlete's foot. Additionally, Calendula's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce redness, itching, and discomfort associated with the condition. remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the most commonly used part, as they contain the highest concentration of anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds.

remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula officinalis flowers in their peak potency during the morning hours when they are fully open.
Dry the flowers in a single layer on a paper towel at room temperature for 2 weeks or use a dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Infuse 1 cup of dried flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil such as jojoba oil or sweet almond oil in a glass jar for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar and discard the solids.
Mix 1/4 cup of the infused oil with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/2 cup of distilled water to create a smooth and consistent cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Azadirachta indica creams helps with athlete's foot because of its potent antifungal properties derived from neem oil. Seeds and leaves are often used together, but if we are forced to pick a third item, then it would be: Buds: They are used to make creams for athlete's foot because they also contain antifungal and antibacterial compounds.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 100g of azadirachta indica leaves in 1 liter of water at 80c for 30 minutes.
Strain the infused liquid and discard the solids to obtain 750ml of extract.
Mix 20g of beeswax with 50g of coconut oil and heat until melted.
Combine 20g of shea butter with the melted beeswax and coconut oil mixture.
Add the azadirachta indica extract to the mixture and stir until well combined.

remedies.remedy_desc: Allium sativum creams helps with athlete's foot because of its antifungal properties, derived from the natural compounds found in garlic. The active ingredients in these creams, such as allicin and ajoene, inhibit the growth of fungi that cause athlete's foot. S-Allylcysteine: It helps with athlete's foot by showing antifungal and antimicrobial activities, which can help combat the causative agents of the infection.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh allium sativum flowers and leaves from a pesticide-free garden and clean them thoroughly.
Infuse 1 cup of the harvested allium sativum in 2 cups of carrier oil like sweet almond oil for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container discard the solids.
Mix 1/2 cup of the infused oil with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of vitamin e oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler over low heat and stir constantly for 10 minutes to achieve a smooth texture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with athlete's foot because of its natural antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties. The gel extracted from the aloe plant has been shown to reduce irritation and discomfort associated with athlete's foot. Its antibacterial properties help to combat fungal infections, promoting a healthy environment for skin recovery. remedy_constituents: Aloin: Aloe barbadensis contains aloin, a anthraquinone glycoside, which helps to treat athlete's foot by its antifungal properties, inhibiting the growth of fungi that cause the infection.
Aloe-emodin: Aloe-emodin is a anthraquinone compound that helps to combat athlete's foot by its potent antifungal activity, which prevents the spread of fungal infections.

remedy_recipe: Weigh 100 grams of aloe barbadensis gel and 20 grams of beeswax for the base mixture.
Melt the beeswax in a double boiler at 160°f for 10 minutes to ensure complete dissolution.
Combine 20 grams of shea butter and 20 grams of coconut oil with the melted beeswax.
Add 5 grams of vitamin e oil and 5 grams of preservative to the mixture and blend well.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it set at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera creams helps with athlete's foot because of its anti-fungal and soothing properties. The gel from the Aloe vera plant contains compounds like aloe-emodin and lupeol, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi that cause athlete's foot. Aloe vera creams also provide a cooling and moisturizing effect on the skin, reducing itching, inflammation, and discomfort associated with athlete's foot. Aloin: Aloein's antifungal and antibacterial properties help to combat the fungal and bacterial infections that cause athlete's foot, promoting a healthy environment for the skin.

Furofuran lactones: These compounds have been shown to exhibit antifungal properties, which help to inhibit the growth of fungi that cause athlete's foot, reducing the severity of symptoms and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera gel extracted from its leaves is commonly used in creams for athlete's foot due to its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties.

remedy_recipe: Weigh 200g of aloe vera gel and mix it with 100g of coconut oil in a double boiler at low heat.
Combine 50g of beeswax with 20g of shea butter and 10g of vitamin e oil in the double boiler.
Heat the mixture of beeswax and shea butter until it reaches a temperature of 180f to 190f.
Slowly add the aloe vera and coconut oil mixture to the beeswax mixture while whisking constantly for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and solidify for at least 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus creams helps with athlete's foot because of its antifungal properties, which target the fungi responsible for the infection. remedy_constituents: Citral: A natural antifungal agent that helps combat fungal infections causing athlete's foot by inhibiting the growth of fungal cells.
Limonene: An antiseptic and antimicrobial compound that helps reduce fungal and bacterial loads on the skin, contributing to the management of athlete's foot.
Geraniol: An antifungal agent with antibacterial properties that helps soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing fungal infections and inflammation associated with athlete's foot.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high oil content, particularly citral, which has antifungal properties that help combat fungal infections like athlete's foot.

Stems: Stems are sometimes used due to their lower cost and availability, and they also contain essential oils with antifungal properties that help combat fungal infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cymbopogon citratus leaves in the early morning after the dew has evaporated and before the heat of the day intensifies.
Steam distill cymbopogon citratus leaves in a distillation apparatus at a temperature between 100 to 150 degrees celsius for 3 hours.
Mix 10 grams of the cymbopogon citratus essential oil with 100 grams of a natural moisturizing base in a clean mixing bowl.
Blend the mixture thoroughly using a hand mixer until it is well combined and a consistent texture is achieved.
Pour the mixture into small containers and store them in a cool dark place for 24 to 48 hours before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Origanum vulgare creams helps with athlete's foot because of its antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The active compounds in Origanum vulgare, such as carvacrol and thymol, have been shown to effectively combat fungal infections that cause athlete's foot. These natural ingredients help to reduce inflammation, soothe itchy skin, and prevent the spread of the infection. By promoting a healthy balance of skin flora, Origanum vulgare creams provide a natural solution for treating and preventing athlete's foot. remedy_constituents: Thymol: Exhibits antifungal and antibacterial properties, helping to control the growth of fungi and bacteria that cause athlete's foot.
Bornyl acetate: Possesses antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with athlete's foot.
Caryophyllene oxide: Displays antifungal and antimicrobial properties, contributing to the suppression of fungal growth and potential relief from athlete's foot symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help combat fungal infections causing athlete's foot.
Stems: Employed for their antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which aid in preventing the growth of fungal pathogens.
Flowers: Utilized for their antifungal and antibacterial properties, as well as their ability to soothe and calm irritated skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of fresh origanum vulgare leaves and flowers from the plant's foliage in the early morning.
Dry the harvested origanum vulgare leaves and flowers in a cool dark place for 7-10 days.
Combine 10g of the dried origanum vulgare leaves and flowers with 100g of sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Steep the mixture for 4-6 hours or until the oil reaches a temperature of 40°c to 60°c.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and transfer the resulting herbal infusion into a clean container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams helps with athlete's foot because of its potent antifungal properties, which combat the underlying fungal infection. Turmeric's active compound, curcumin, possesses excellent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, reducing redness, itching, and scaling associated with athlete's foot. Turmerone: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and antifungal effects, reducing the severity of symptoms associated with athlete's foot, including redness and itching.

Demethoxycurcumin: This polyphenolic compound has been shown to exhibit antifungal activity against various fungal pathogens, including those responsible for athlete's foot, and may also help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress.

Barks: Barks are not typically used for creams in this context, but I found some information about this one.
remedy_recipe: Melt 200g of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat for 15 minutes.
Combine 150g of coconut oil and 100g of shea butter in a mixing bowl.
Add 50g of curcuma longa powder and 20g of vitamin e oil to the mixing bowl.
Blend the mixture for 5 minutes using a hand mixer on high speed.
Pour the mixture into sterilized containers and let it cool and solidify completely.
